extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE won a bunch sprint at 187km after BALLERINI's breakaway caught at 3km, exploiting sprinter fatigue and RICHÁRD's leadout
tactical_patterns: BALLERINI survived in break until 3km despite peloton hovering 37 seconds back, revealing successful gap management under pressure.
ALAPHILIPPE positioned as final leadout man for VIVIANI then opportunistically sprinted himself, exposing GC rider versatility in tired peloton.
RICHEZE delivered final pull for Quick-Step but ALAPHILIPPE on his wheel capitalized, showing designated sprinter VIVIANI was mispositioned.
Stage 6 fatigue allowed non-sprinter ALAPHILIPPE to win bunch sprint, indicating pure sprinters lose speed advantage in multi-day races.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: gained 10 bonus seconds in GC while winning proper bunch sprint, demonstrating rare versatility when specialists fatigued
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: positive: survived in breakaway until 3km to go despite peloton at sub-2min gap, showing strong breakaway resilience
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: mispositioned in own team's leadout while ALAPHILIPPE took wheel of RICHEZE, revealing sprint train coordination failure
rider_signals.RICHEZE Maximiliano: positive: delivered massive final pull for Quick-Step despite ALAPHILIPPE rather than VIVIANI capitalizing on positioning
